title: Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints
exclusions: Does NOT validate key rotation (covered by 22.41.3); focuses on transport posture at backup ingress.
evidence: Saved search 'backup_tls_posture' (daily) reports any backup repository endpoint with legacy protocol or near-expiry certificate.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.12 backup policies include protection of transport.
HIPAA transmission security applies to backup ingress.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Backup repo at backup.example.com negotiates TLS 1.0; the UC fires daily.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All backup endpoints negotiate TLS 1.2/1.3 with strong ciphers; no alert.

dataSources: TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory.
spl:
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-24h
| search host IN (`backup_repository_endpoints`)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<45 OR protocol="TLSv1.0" OR protocol="TLSv1.1" OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
description: Ensures the transport to backup repositories — traditionally neglected — matches the same TLS posture as customer-facing systems.
value: Closes a gap pattern observed in DORA inspections where the backup plane had older TLS than the production plane.
implementation: (1) Inventory backup endpoints; (2) scan daily; (3) on finding, ticket to platform engineering.
visualization: Table of endpoints with weak posture, time chart of scan results, single-value '% of backup endpoints with strong TLS'.
knownFalsePositives: Appliance-managed endpoints with vendor-pinned TLS libraries may need a supply-chain exception.
